mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth is an always-on labor-income trend signal: stronger 3-month payroll gains raise household income expectations and near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not predict higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, with walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates and raises refinancing costs before earnings adjust, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while FEDFUNDS is not rising, household demand expands without an offsetting discount-rate shock, improving revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises over 3 months while FEDFUNDS change over 3 months is <=0.00pp
evidence_refs: fact:Latest snapshot reports RSAFS=375473.0 and FEDFUNDS=1.81
regime_hint: demand recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform cases with rising RSAFS alone by at least 25bp average 60-day return in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening factory output and operating leverage, reducing expected cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or by returns above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS also rises, inflation pressure and policy tightening jointly compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises >0.6% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS rises >0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Latest snapshot reports CPIAUCSL=218.88 and FEDFUNDS=1.81
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS change has nonnegative sign for 60-day returns in a rolling walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A strong increase in HOUST signals improving credit availability and future durable-goods demand, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST increases have a lower average 60-day S&P 500 return than non-triggered periods in more than two consecutive out-of-sample years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, causing equity investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or show no negative event-study abnormal return.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is expanding while UNRATE remains elevated, the economy is in an early recovery phase where operating leverage improves from depressed labor conditions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises over 2 quarters and UNRATE is >6.0
evidence_refs: fact:Latest snapshot reports GDPC1=11727.4 and UNRATE=6.1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-growth and high-UNRATE condition does not deliver higher 60-day returns than high-UNRATE periods without GDPC1 growth.
